---
title: "![](images/icons/icon_14.png){.title-icon} Choosing a statistical analysis"
subtitle: "Overview: which test or model fits your data?"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-warning}
**Under development.** The table is a quick overview. An interactive "click your way through" version is on the way.
:::

Which analysis you need depends mainly on the **data type** (numeric, binary or time-to-event), whether the groups are **paired or unpaired**, and whether the assumptions for a **parametric** test hold (otherwise a nonparametric one). The table below matches each situation to a test/model and the corresponding **R** command.

::: {.callout-note}
The R commands are base R (the `stats` package) unless noted otherwise: `survival` for time-to-event and `epitools` for risk/odds ratios. Regression and time-to-event are expanded in [Regression](13c_regression.qmd) and [Time-to-event](13d_time-to-event.qmd). Confirm that extra packages are installed on DST.
:::

---

## Numeric / continuous data

| Purpose | Paired? | Type | Test | Assumptions (brief) | R |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Compare one group to a hypothetical value | Unpaired | Parametric | One-sample t-test | Normal distribution, independent obs. | `t.test(x, mu = m0)` |
| Compare one group to a hypothetical value | Unpaired | Nonparametric | Wilcoxon signed-rank | Independent, same distribution | `wilcox.test(x, mu = m0)` |
| Compare two unpaired groups | Unpaired | Parametric | Unpaired t-test | Normal in both, (equal sd), independent | `t.test(y ~ group)` (Welch; `var.equal = TRUE` for equal variance) |
| Compare two unpaired groups | Unpaired | Nonparametric | Mann-Whitney (Wilcoxon rank-sum) | Independent, same distribution shape | `wilcox.test(y ~ group)` |
| Compare two paired groups | Paired | Parametric | Paired t-test | Normally distributed paired differences | `t.test(x1, x2, paired = TRUE)` |
| Compare two paired groups | Paired | Nonparametric | Wilcoxon signed-rank | Independent paired differences, same distribution | `wilcox.test(x1, x2, paired = TRUE)` |
| General linear model for the mean | Unpaired | Parametric | Linear regression | Normal distribution, independent obs. | `lm(y ~ x)` |
| Compare several means | Unpaired | Parametric | One-way ANOVA | Normal distribution, independent obs. | `aov(y ~ group)` or `oneway.test(y ~ group, var.equal = TRUE)` |
| Compare several means | Unpaired | Nonparametric | Kruskal-Wallis | Independent, same distribution | `kruskal.test(y ~ group)` |

## Binary / dichotomous data

| Purpose | Paired? | Type | Test | Assumptions (brief) | R |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Compare one group to a hypothetical value | Unpaired | Parametric | Binomial test | Binomial distribution, independent obs. | `binom.test(x, n, p = p0)` (exact); `prop.test(x, n, p = p0)` (approx.) |
| Compare two unpaired groups | Unpaired | - | Chi-square / Fisher's exact | Independent obs. (Fisher for small counts) | `chisq.test(table)`; `fisher.test(table)` |
| Describe risk difference, RR or OR (with CI) | Unpaired | - | Descriptive measures | - | `epitools::riskratio()`, `epitools::oddsratio()` |
| Correlation / trend | Unpaired | Nonparametric | Spearman rank correlation | - | `cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman")` |
| Compare two paired groups | Paired | Parametric | McNemar | Paired obs., independent pairs | `mcnemar.test(table)` |
| Binary regression for relative risk | Unpaired | Parametric | Log-binomial regression | Binomial distribution; probability modelled by covariates | `glm(y ~ x, family = binomial(link = "log"))` |

## Time to event

| Purpose | Paired? | Type | Test/model | Assumptions (brief) | R |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Estimate the cumulative risk / compare groups | Unpaired | Nonparametric | Kaplan-Meier + log-rank | Independent obs., independent right censoring | `survfit(Surv(time, event) ~ group)`; `survdiff(Surv(time, event) ~ group)` |
| Compare rates (hazard ratio) | Unpaired | Semi-parametric | Cox regression | Independent obs., independent right censoring, proportional hazards | `coxph(Surv(time, event) ~ x)`; PH check: `cox.zph()` |

::: {.callout-note}
Remember: anything leaving DST must go through **output control** - no small cells, only aggregated results. See [Phase 14 - Export and repatriation](14_eksport-hjemsendelse.qmd).
:::
